title: What is a Super Clone Watch? A Buyer's Guide
excerpt: Understanding the tiers of replica watches — and what separates a Super Clone from the rest.
author: Clonicawatch Editorial
content:
## The Replica Watch Tiers

The world of replica watches spans a wide quality range. At the top sits the "Super Clone" — a category focused on 1:1 fidelity to the original, using matched materials, near-identical movements, and meticulous finishing.

## Key Factories

A handful of specialized ateliers dominate the super clone space:

- **Clean Factory** — renowned for Rolex Submariner, Daytona, and GMT-Master II
- **APF Factory** — the benchmark for Audemars Piguet Royal Oak
- **BP Factory** — a leader in Patek Philippe Nautilus
- **ZF Factory** — known for Panerai and Hublot
- **Noob Factory** — the historical pioneer of the space

## What Makes a Super Clone?

1. **Material fidelity** — 904L stainless steel for Rolex (not 316L), ceramic bezels (not aluminum), sapphire crystals
2. **Movement behavior** — the clone caliber replicates the original's power reserve, beat rate, and jewel count
3. **Dial accuracy** — correct fonts, lume application, texture (Grande Tapisserie on Royal Oak)
4. **Weight & dimensions** — matching the original gram-for-gram

## How to Buy Wisely

- Always ask which factory produced the piece
- Request the movement caliber (e.g., "Clone 3235" vs "Asian 2824")
- Check the seller's packaging — reputable dealers include the branded box set
- Prefer sellers who offer discreet worldwide shipping with tracking

title: Clean vs VS Factory: Which Makes the Better Rolex?
excerpt: Two of the most prolific Rolex super clone factories — head to head on Submariner and GMT.
author: Clonicawatch Editorial
content:
## Clean Factory — The Industry Standard

Clean has built its reputation on precision. Their Submariner 126610LN is widely considered the reference point for super clone Rolex models.

### Strengths
- 904L stainless steel across all models
- Clone 3235 with correct 70h power reserve
- Exceptional ceramic bezel pearl alignment
- Chromalight lume closely matches OE

### Weaknesses
- Higher price point in the super clone market
- Limited model range (no Explorer II as of 2026)

## VS Factory — The Value Alternative

VS has carved out a strong position with aggressive pricing while delivering solid quality.

### Strengths
- Wider model range (including older references)
- More accessible price points
- Strong Sea-Dweller and Deepsea lineup

### Weaknesses
- 316L steel on some models (vs 904L)
- Subtle dial text differences on certain references

## Our Verdict

For the Submariner, GMT-Master II, and Daytona — **Clean is the stronger choice**. For Sea-Dweller, Deepsea, and less-demanded references — **VS offers better value**.

Message us on WhatsApp for factory-specific availability.

title: How to Spot a Low-Quality Replica (and Avoid Getting Burned)
excerpt: Not all replicas are created equal. Here are the red flags that separate factory garbage from super clone quality.
author: Clonicawatch Editorial
content:
## Red Flag #1: "Swiss Movement" Without Specifics

If the seller says "Swiss movement" but won't name the caliber, walk away. Legitimate super clone sellers will tell you exactly what's inside — "Clone 3235" or "Swiss ETA 2836-2" or "Asian 2824".

## Red Flag #2: No Factory Mention

A seller who won't or can't name the factory (Clean, ZF, APF, BP, Noob) is likely selling unbranded generic replicas. The factory matters because quality varies dramatically between ateliers.

## Red Flag #3: Aluminum "Ceramic" Bezel

Modern Rolex sport references use ceramic bezels. If the replica bezel scratches easily or shows brush marks, it's aluminum — not ceramic. A magnet test is the easiest check (ceramic is not magnetic).

## Red Flag #4: Wrong Dial Text

Subtle differences in dial text spacing, font weight, or color give away low-quality replicas. Compare product photos carefully against official brand images before buying.

## Red Flag #5: No Box & Papers

Reputable super clone sellers include a full branded box set, warranty card, and adjustment tools. If these are "$50 extra" or "out of stock", the piece is likely lower tier.

## The Bottom Line

Buy from sellers who provide full specs, name the factory, and ship with complete packaging. In the super clone world, transparency is the best proxy for quality.
